This article details [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] rugby league football club's 2022 season. This is the Saints' 26th consecutive season in the [[Super League]].

==Season review==
On 10 February, St Helens began their Super League campaign against [[Catalans Dragons]]. In a rematch of the [[2021 Super League Grand Final|2021 Grand Final]] they repeated their success, this time with a 28–8 win.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36608/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-1 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 1 |publisher=RFL |date=13 February 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}</ref> This was followed with a 38–6 win [[Hull F.C.]] that saw St Helens move to top of the table on points difference.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36653/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-2 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 2 |publisher=RFL |date=20 February 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}</ref> Their good form continued as they won their opening five games<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36784/betfred-super-league-round-five-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round Five Round-Up|publisher=RFL |date=13 March 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}</ref> before suffering an unexpected 22–20 away loss to [[Toulouse Olympique]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36835/betfred-super-league-round-six-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round Six Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=20 March 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}</ref> In April, St Helens faced Catalans Dragons in the quarter-finals of the [[Challenge Cup]] and progressed with a 36–20 win.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/37006/betfred-challenge-cup-quarter-final-round-up |title=Betfred Challenge Cup quarter-final round-up |publisher=RFL |date=10 April 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}</ref> They then reinforced their position at the top of the league with wins over [[Wigan Warriors]] and [[Huddersfield Giants]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/37073/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-easter-monday |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Easter Monday |publisher=RFL |date=18 April 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}</ref> In the semi-finals of the Challenge Cup, St Helens lost to Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/37226/wigan-fight-back-to-clinch-challenge-cup-final-spot-in-elland-road-classic |title=Wigan fight back to clinch Challenge Cup final spot in Elland Road classic |publisher=RFL |date=7 May 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}</ref> Meanwhile, they continued to perform well in the league and a 13–12 win over [[Wakefield Trinity]] in July moved them six points clear at the top of the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60566/betfred-super-league-round-20-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round 20 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=24 July 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}</ref>

At the end of August, St Helens suffered their only home loss of the season, 34–18 to Wakefield, but despite this were confirmed as [[League Leaders' Shield|league leaders']] as Wigan also lost.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60825/betfred-super-league-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}}publisher=RFL |date=30 August 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}</ref> The final round of the regular season was a top against bottom clash in which St Helens won 36–16 over an already relegated Toulouse.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60859/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-27 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 27 |publisher=RFL |date=3 September 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}</ref> As league leaders', St Helens began their play-off campaign at the semi-finals stage with a 19–12 home win over [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60938/leeds-and-st-helens-to-meet-in-betfred-super-league-grand-final |title=Leeds and St Helens to meet in Betfred Super League Grand Final |publisher=RFL |date=18 September 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}</ref> At the 2022 [[Man of Steel Awards|Super League Awards]] on 20 September, [[Jack Welsby]] was named the Young Player of the Year.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60957/brodie-croft-named-steve-prescott-mbe-man-of-steel-for-2022 |title=Brodie Croft named Steve Prescott MBE Man of Steel for 2022 |publisher=RFL |date=21 September 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}</ref> On 24 September, St Helens won 24–12 against [[Leeds Rhinos]] to become the first club to win four consecutive Super League Grand Finals. [[Jonny Lomax]] was named as the [[Harry Sunderland Trophy|man of the match]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60978/saints-make-history-by-completing-four-peat |title=Saints make history by completing four-peat |publisher=RFL |date=24 September 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}</ref>
